note: Version 2 — updated to reflect PRD revision of 2026-04-07. Role hierarchy expanded, permanent group membership added, team rotation and auto-suggestions added, request-to-serve flow replaces invitation model. Features requiring revision reset to passes=false.

features.name: Authentication and role access
features.description: Establish email/password authentication and enforce the expanded six-role hierarchy with correct scoped access for all protected surfaces.
features.revisionNote: Add all_depts_leader, team_head, supporter roles; update RLS policies, route guards, and role-based entry points for all six roles. Scope: structural foundation only. Supporter permission mirroring is owned by RS-F018. Event-creation grant is owned by RS-F002.
steps: Support six roles: super_admin, all_depts_leader, dept_head, team_head, supporter, volunteer in the database enum and TypeScript type system.
Route each role into a role-appropriate entry experience on sign-in.
Enforce role-scoped access across all protected views and data using baseline RLS policies.
Provide dashboard stubs for all_depts_leader, team_head, and supporter; full dashboard content is covered by RS-F010.

features.name: Event lifecycle management
features.description: Allow Super Admin and All Departments Leaders to create and manage events; Dept Heads and Team Heads may create events only if granted access by Super Admin.
features.revisionNote: Restrict event creation to super_admin and all_depts_leader by default. Add grant mechanism for dept_head and team_head.
steps: Restrict event creation to Super Admin and All Departments Leader by default.
Implement a grant mechanism allowing Super Admin to give dept_head or team_head event-creation access.
Maintain event lifecycle states: draft, published, completed.
Ensure downstream planning records remain anchored to an event.

features.name: Department and team structure
features.description: Model departments and named teams so ownership, permanent group membership, and visibility follow the real church planning hierarchy.
features.revisionNote: Rename sub-teams to teams. Add rotation labels (A/B/C), headcount requirements per team per event type, permanent membership model. Team Head is optional at team level.
steps: Model named teams within departments; a team is independent of whether a Team Head is assigned.
Support optional rotation labels (A, B, C) per team for rotation scheduling.
Support headcount requirements per team per event type, set by Dept Head or All Departments Leader.
Allow volunteers to belong to a team or report directly to a Dept Head with no team.
Team Heads always sit under a Dept Head unless moved or removed by an authorized leader.
Restrict structure management to authorized planning scopes.

features.name: Volunteer onboarding and profile setup
features.description: Guide new volunteers through the minimum setup needed to submit a request to join a department and participate in the rostering system.
features.revisionNote: Onboarding department interest now creates a formal request to join (RS-F006 flow) rather than a loose interest signal. Update onboarding outcome to reflect the permanent membership model.
steps: Gate new volunteer users into an onboarding path before the main dashboard.
Capture availability preferences, department interest as a formal request to join, and optional skill input.
Transition completed volunteers into the role-appropriate dashboard experience.

features.name: Availability and blockout management
features.description: Allow volunteers to maintain service availability and make that information usable during roster planning and cross-team auto-suggestions.
steps: Capture dated availability plus preference or blockout signals.
Make availability editable over time for volunteers.
Surface relevant availability inside leader planning views and auto-suggestion logic.

features.name: Request to join and permanent group membership
features.description: Allow volunteers to request to join departments; approval creates permanent group membership and optionally places them into a named team.
features.revisionNote: Previous feature was event-scoped interest requests. Now approval creates permanent group membership. Add team placement step after approval. Membership persists across all events.
steps: Let volunteers submit a request to join a department.
Route each request to the appropriate Dept Head for review.
On approval, create permanent group membership for the volunteer in that department.
Allow Dept Head to optionally place the approved volunteer into a named team.
Reflect membership in all leader views regardless of active events.

features.name: Skill profile and approval
features.description: Capture volunteer skills and require approval by Dept Head or Team Head before those skills affect planning.
features.revisionNote: Super Admin now creates skills via manual entry or bulk template upload. Both Dept Head and Team Head can approve, edit, or remove volunteer skill claims.
steps: Allow Super Admin to define skills via manual entry or bulk template upload.
Allow volunteers to add skills to their profiles (pending approval).
Allow both Dept Heads and Team Heads to approve, edit, or remove volunteer skill claims within their scope.
Ensure only approved skills feed planning and gap detection logic.

features.name: Roster planning and request-to-serve flow
features.description: Give Dept Heads a structured way to select a team for an event and send requests to serve to Team Heads and their volunteers.
features.revisionNote: Replace individual assignment creation with team-selection and request-to-serve flow. Add Team Head confirmation step. Add substitute Team Head selection when a Team Head declines.
steps: Allow Dept Head to select which team serves a given event (manually or via rotation label).
Send a request to serve to the selected Team Head and all team members.
Allow Team Heads to confirm or decline their team's request.
Allow volunteers to confirm or decline their individual request.
If a Team Head declines, allow Dept Head to assign a substitute Team Head from another team.
Highlight non-confirmations on the Dept Head dashboard.
Treat removal of a request as a high-impact action requiring confirmation.

features.name: Skill-gap and headcount-gap detection
features.description: Surface skill coverage and headcount shortfalls before the event so leaders can act in time.
features.revisionNote: Add headcount gap detection alongside skill gap detection. Headcount requirements are set per team per event type in RS-F003.
steps: Compare confirmed coverage against required skills per team per event.
Compare confirmed headcount against required headcount per team per event type.
Surface both skill and headcount gap signals in leader planning views.
Ensure gap detection uses only approved skills.

features.name: Personalized weekly dashboard
features.description: Provide each of the six roles with a weekly operational dashboard showing only the context they need.
features.revisionNote: Add dashboard views for all_depts_leader, team_head, and supporter. Highlight non-confirmations. Show permanent membership context to leaders outside of event context.
steps: Build role-specific weekly dashboard states for all six roles.
Populate dashboards with scoped requests to serve, planning health, non-confirmations, and relevant context.
Highlight non-confirming Team Heads and volunteers for Dept Heads.
Reflect request-to-serve and planning state changes quickly enough for weekly coordination.

features.name: Instructions and media sharing
features.description: Let leaders publish event-specific guidance, notes, and attachments to the right audience.
steps: Create the instruction publishing flow tied to event, department, or team context.
Support attachment of images and documents.
Restrict visibility to the intended recipients.

features.name: Request-to-serve response workflow
features.description: Close the loop between a request to serve and volunteer or Team Head confirmation with controlled status changes.
steps: Provide confirm and decline actions from request-to-serve experiences.
Require confirmation dialog on decline.
Enforce controlled status progression: requested → confirmed / declined → served.
Reflect response outcomes back into leader and volunteer weekly views.

features.name: Notifications and scheduled alerts
features.description: Notify volunteers and leaders when request-to-serve state changes and send pre-event operational alerts.
steps: Trigger notifications for request-to-serve issuance and response changes.
Schedule and send the required 2-day and 5-day leader alerts.
Prevent stale or invalid notification delivery.

features.name: Admin oversight, soft delete, and approval controls
features.description: Route destructive actions through soft delete and Super Admin review so operational safety is preserved.
steps: Route destructive changes through a soft-delete state.
Create the Super Admin review path for permanent removal decisions.
Keep inactive records out of normal operations while preserving review visibility.

features.name: Error handling and support escalation
features.description: Make failures understandable and recoverable with calm messaging, clear next steps, and support escalation paths.
steps: Define calm, actionable error states across core user flows.
Add leader contact and bug-report escalation paths where required.
Prevent failure states from leaking protected data or breaking scope rules.

features.name: Team rotation scheduling
features.description: Allow Dept Heads to schedule automatic or manual team rotation across recurring events.
steps: Allow Dept Heads to assign rotation labels (A, B, C) to teams within a department.
Support automatic rotation — cycle through teams in label order across consecutive events of the same type.
Allow manual override — Dept Head selects a specific team for any event without disrupting the rotation sequence.
Show the rotation schedule on the Dept Head dashboard.

features.name: Cross-team auto-suggestions for gap filling
features.description: When a team member cannot confirm, surface ranked replacement suggestions from other teams in the same department.
steps: Generate ranked replacement suggestions from other teams in the same department when a gap exists.
Rank suggestions by skill match and volunteer availability.
Exclude volunteers already assigned to the event.
Allow Dept Head to confirm a suggestion — Dept Head confirmation always required; no auto-assignment.

features.name: Supporter and secretary role management
features.description: Allow Super Admin to assign a Supporter to a specific leader so the Supporter can act on that leader's behalf.
steps: Allow Super Admin to assign a Supporter to a specific leader.
Mirror the assigned leader's operational permissions to the Supporter, excluding admin-level access.
Attribute actions taken by a Supporter to that Supporter in audit contexts.
Restrict each Supporter to one assigned leader at a time.
